Sep..personality: A tall, slim, sallow woman who has become emotionally flat and intellectually dulled, often appearing prematurely aged. She is irritable, obstinate, permits no opposition, and vents spite on those she loves best, yet simultaneously dreads being alone. Her temperament swings unpredictably between yielding gentleness and disagreeable obstinacy.
Sep..mental_state: The mind is sluggish, stupid, and forgetful, with slow thinking and an inability to perform mental labor. She may appear intoxicated or benumbed, answering questions only in monosyllables when pressed, and sits in taciturn melancholy saying nothing. There is fear of insanity, fear of poverty, fear of ghosts, and a sense that unseen figures fill the atmosphere around her.
Sep..emotional_pattern: The most striking feature is the abolition of natural affection — she knows she ought to love her husband and children but feels nothing, as if all capacity for warmth has been stilled. She finds no joy in life, all things seem strange and unreal, and she is worse in company yet dreads solitude. This emotional deadness alternates with spells of weeping, sarcasm, and a compulsion to annoy others and relate her grievances.
